title: Projects
text:
Erika C. Freeman

  

  

    About

    Projects

    Publications

    Protocols

    CV

    Contact

  








Projects


Projects

  
    
    
      An Ecology of Molecules
      Treating the thousands of molecules in a drop of water as a living community. It's an emerging field that brings ecological thinking to the chemistry of ecosystems, and one I'm glad to help shape.
    
  
  
    
    
      Predictive Ecology
      Forecasting ecosystems, not just describing them. I co-lead work that turns rich environmental data into early signals of how carbon and water quality will shift as the world changes.
    
  
  
    
    
      Precision Biogeochemistry
      Teaching AI to read water chemistry. My pipelines identify thousands of molecules in a single sample at once, turning dense mass-spectrometry data into meaning faster and more reliably than anyone could by hand.
    
  
  
    
    
      Carbon on the Move
      Following carbon from the forest floor into the water. When land-use change washes carbon out of soils, it rewires the chemistry of rivers and lakes, and I trace what that means for the whole ecosystem.
    
  
  
    
    
      Carbon in Practice
      Where the science meets real climate decisions. From carbon footprinting at a food-tech start-up to corporate environmental work, I connect molecular science to how carbon is measured, verified, and acted on.
    
  
  
    
    
      A Kinder Academia
      Making academic life more humane. From a Science essay on having children in academia (with Cecilia Padilla-Iglesias) to ongoing work on equity and mentorship.
    
  


Off the clock? Personal projects → (private — write me for the password)

title:
Dr.
      Erika C.
      Freeman
      Carbon Biogeochemist
text:
Bio

I’m Erika, a researcher at the Leibniz-Institut für Gewässerökologie und Binnenfischerei (IGB) in Berlin. I study how carbon moves through aquatic systems and what this means for human wellbeing. Most of my days go to the ecology of molecules, an idea I love that brings ecological thinking to molecular biogeochemistry.
Before IGB I trained at some wonderful places: a postdoc at Eawag, the ETH Domain’s Swiss Federal Institute of Aquatic Science and Technology, and a PhD in biogeochemistry (2024) at the University of Cambridge, where I was a Gates Cambridge Fellow. I’m grateful to the brilliant people who shaped me along the way, and just as happy to have taken what they taught me and run somewhere of my own.
Outside the lab I’ve worn a few other hats too: life-cycle assessments at a UK food-tech start-up (Foodsteps) and corporate waste management back in Canada (Hydro One).
And when I’m not at the office, you’ll usually find me doing irresponsible amounts of running and climbing, or out exploring the great out-the-door with my partner.

title: Erika C. Freeman
text:
Current position
Junior Group Leader (Tenure Track) — group Aquatic Biogeochemistry of Carbon (ABC Lab), Leibniz-Institute of Freshwater Ecology and Inland Fisheries (IGB), Department 1 (Ecohydrology & Biogeochemistry); Programme Area 4. Since January 2026. Research programme: the ecology of molecules — dissolved organic matter (DOM) at molecular resolution (FT-ICR-MS) to predict and manage carbon-climate feedbacks and water quality.

title: Erika C. Freeman
text:
Research funding & analytical awards

DOE EMSL CAM #62138 (Molecular Fingerprints of Metal Fate), sole PI — approved Feb 2026; free PNNL FT-ICR-MS / NMR / multi-omics access.
DOE EMSL FICUS #62211 (AI-Enabled Phytomining of Critical Minerals), co-PI with N. Jafari (Texas A&M) — invited to full proposal, Mar 2026.
IGB start-up budget €50,000.
Applications in preparation: DFG Sachbeihilfe TRACE (Sept 2026); DFG Emmy Noether / FreshDreaMS; ERC Starting Grant WATERMARK (2027); Leibniz Junior Research Group VITALS (Mar 2028).

    CV

Protocols


protocols
Methods from the lab, released openly on protocols.io as they are finalized — each with a citable DOI. DOIs appear here as protocols go live.



Planned


DOM sampling and preservation for FT-ICR-MS protocols.io (planned) Amber-vial, three-quarter fill, anti-crack freezing; connector-leaching QA. Adapted from the DOMseasons protocol (Tanentzap lab).


Solid-phase extraction (PPL) of dissolved organic matter protocols.io (planned) Acidification, PPL cartridge conditioning, elution and drying for FT-ICR-MS.


FT-ICR-MS acquisition and quality control for DOM protocols.io (planned) Instrument settings, calibration, blanks and QC for ultrahigh-resolution DOM.


Molecular formula assignment and data processing protocols.io (planned) Peak picking, formula assignment, filtering and QC of FT-ICR-MS spectra.


AI-assisted molecular and structural annotation pipeline protocols.io (planned) Machine-learning annotation of DOM spectra; sequenced to complement the structural-annotation paper and FreshDreaMS.
